title: Molecular Epidemiology, prenatal exposure and prevention of cancer.
ehr_evidence: The study uses personal air monitors and laboratory biomarkers (urinary metabolites, blood tests) rather than EHR.
summary: A discussion on molecular epidemiology focusing on prenatal exposure to environmental carcinogens, specifically PAHs, and their role in fetal DNA damage and chromosomal aberrations.
key_findings: Fetal susceptibility to procarcinogenic DNA damage is high.
High prenatal PAH exposure is associated with a higher frequency of stable chromosomal aberrations in cord blood.
Maternal and cord white blood cells had equivalent levels of PAH-DNA adducts despite lower fetal dose.
population: Pregnant mothers and newborns in New York City
